title: abutilon polyandrum
intro: Abutilon polyandrum, also known as the Indian mallow or country mallow, is a plant species native to tropical Africa and Asia, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and malva-like leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been utilized to treat various health conditions such as fever, cough, and skin irritations. The health benefits of Abutilon polyandrum include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and support the immune system, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. In herbal preparations, the leaves and roots of the plant are often used in infusions, decoctions, or as a paste to treat various ailments, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
